fresh_facts.claim: Thousands more US ground forces are heading to the Gulf region amid a stalemate, with President Trump signaling potential wind-down of operations.
Iran's ballistic missile stockpile remains about half intact since early April ceasefire.
IRGC gunboats fired on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran renewed Hormuz attacks despite ceasefire extension, with earlier strikes hitting US-linked Gulf sites with missiles, drones, and jets.

Unconfirmed reports indicate the IRGC, led by Commander Ahmad Vahidi, has detained or sidelined President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and Foreign Minister Araghchi.
Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf was removed on April 22.
The IRGC displayed a Kheibar Shekan (likely Kornamshahr-4) missile in Revolution Square on April 22 as a show of dominance.
Supreme Leader Khamenei's reported coma leaves authority fragmented, with IRGC consolidating under hardliners like Vahidi.
A three-member Provisional Leadership Council formed post-attacks, comprising Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Arafi.
The Assembly of Experts' succession process stalled after an Israeli strike in Qom on March 3.
Trump posted on Truth Social on April 28 that Iran is in a 'state of collapse' and must 'figure out their leadership,' rejecting a non-nuclear peace proposal.
US extended a ceasefire at Pakistan's request but maintains Hormuz blockade.
Hardline Iranian lawmakers aligned with Saeed Jalili boycotted support for negotiators led by Ghalibaf.
Putin pledged Russian support for Iran on April 28.
IRGC fired on a tanker and reimposed Hormuz blockade on April 18, reversing a reopening announcement.
Aramco extended LPG shipment suspension due to unrepaired Hormuz damage on April 28.
Flights resumed between Tehran and Moscow after a two-month halt, signaling some economic ties amid blockade.
Iran's Supreme National Security Council met on Tuesday April 28 over fears of renewed protests.
UK summoned Iran's ambassador over 'unacceptable' embassy posts on April 28.
US intelligence advisers noted Iran's disunity amid internal power struggles.
Iran's Supreme National Security Council Secretary Zolghadr was unable to coordinate between IRGC and civilian leadership as of April 22.
Iran clashed with the US at the UN over Hormuz closure.
Ceasefire talks stalled over US naval blockade, with Iran rejecting proposals as of April 6.
Iran projects confidence in military capability, rejecting direct US talks and conditioning negotiations via Pakistan mediation as of late April.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a compounding crisis marked by fragmented leadership (Provisional Leadership Council amid Supreme Leader incapacity), renewed Hormuz attacks despite ceasefire, and intensifying internal power struggles between civilian authorities and hardline IRGC elements. Trump signals potential operation wind-down while maintaining blockade and rejecting peace proposals, while Russia pledges support and economic lifelines remain tenuous amid Aramco suspension.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 28, 2026, Trump declared Iran in 'state of collapse' and rejected non-nuclear peace proposals, Putin pledged Russian support, UK summoned Iran's ambassador over diplomatic incidents, and Aramco suspended LPG shipments due to Hormuz damage—marking consolidated international diplomatic response alongside economic pressure intensification.
